web_text: In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God. The same was in the beginning with God. All things were made through him. Without him was not anything made that has been made. In him was life, and the life was the light of men.
slang_text: Way, way back, before there was even a "before," the Word was already kickin' it with God -- and check this out, the Word WAS God. Fer sure. He was tight with God since the dawn of time, no lie. Everything that's ever existed got made through Him -- there's not one bogus thing out there that popped up without Him. Life itself was pumping through Him, and that life was like a righteous light shining on every last one of us.

web_text:
The light shines in the darkness, and the darkness hasn’t overcome
 it. There came a man, sent from God, whose name was John. The same came as a witness, that he might testify about the light, that all might believe through him. He was not the light, but was sent that he might testify about the light.
slang_text: That light is blazing right through the darkness, and the darkness never could snuff it out, no way, no how. Then this dude rolls up, sent straight from God, goes by John. He showed up as a witness, to spill the truth about the light so everybody could believe 'cause of him. He wasn't the light himself, though -- he just got sent to point folks toward it, like a boss.

web_text: The true light that enlightens everyone was coming into the world. He was in the world, and the world was made through him, and the world didn’t recognize him. He came to his own, and those who were his own didn’t receive him. But as many as received him, to them he gave the right to become God’s children, to those who believe in his name:
slang_text: The genuine, righteous light, the one that lights up every last person, was headed into the world. He was already out there in it -- a world that only exists 'cause He made it, go figure -- and the world didn't have a clue who he was. He came home to his own crew, and they gave him the cold shoulder too, way harsh. But for everybody who did take him in, who put their trust in his name, he handed them the right to become God's own kids.

web_text: who were born not of blood, nor of the will of the flesh, nor of the will of man, but of God. The Word became flesh, and lived among us. We saw his glory, such glory as of the one and only Son of the Father, full of grace and truth. John testified about him. He cried out, saying, “This was he of whom I said, ‘He who comes after me has surpassed me, for he was before me.’” From his fullness we all received grace upon grace.
slang_text: and that's not outta blood, or whatever people wanted, or some guy's say-so -- that's straight from God. The Word turned to flesh and moved in right here among us. We saw his glory with our own eyes, the epic kind of glory only the one and only Son of the Father's got, totally loaded with grace and truth. John testified about him, calling out, "This is the one I meant when I said, 'The dude coming after me is already major-league ahead of me, 'cause he was around way before me.'" And outta everything he's got, we all keep scoring blessing on top of blessing, grace stacked on grace.

web_text: For the law was given through Moses. Grace and truth were realized through Jesus Christ. No one has seen God at any time. The one and only Son, who is in the bosom of the Father, he has declared him. This is John’s testimony, when the Jews sent priests and Levites from Jerusalem to ask him, “Who are you?” He declared, and didn’t deny, but he declared, “I am not the Christ.”
slang_text: See, the law came down through Moses, but grace and truth got real through Jesus Christ. Nobody's ever laid eyes on God, not once, no joke. But the one and only Son, tight in the Father's corner, he's the one who let us in on it. This is John's testimony -- when the Jews sent priests and Levites all the way from Jerusalem to grill him, "Who are you, anyway?" He didn't dodge it, didn't fib one bit, told 'em straight: "I'm not the Christ, no way."

web_text:
They asked him, “What then? Are you Elijah?”
He said, “I am not.”
“Are you the prophet?”
He answered, “No.” They said therefore to him, “Who are you? Give us an answer to take back to those who sent us. What do you say about yourself?” He said, “I am the voice of one crying in the wilderness, ‘Make straight the way of the Lord,’ as Isaiah the prophet said.” The ones who had been sent were from the Pharisees.
slang_text:
So they hit him with, "Okay, then what -- you Elijah?"
He said, "Nope, not me."
"You the prophet, then?"
He said, "Negative." So they pushed harder, "Then who are you, dude? We gotta haul an answer back to the guys who sent us. What's the story on you?" He told them, "I'm the voice hollering out in the wilderness, 'Make the Lord's road straight,' just like the prophet Isaiah called it, fer sure." The ones who got sent were from the Pharisees.

web_text: They asked him, “Why then do you baptize, if you are not the Christ, nor Elijah, nor the prophet?” John answered them, “I baptize in water, but among you stands one whom you don’t know. He is the one who comes after me, who is preferred before me, whose sandal strap I’m not worthy to loosen.” These things were done in Bethany beyond the Jordan, where John was baptizing.
slang_text: They came back with, "Then why are you out here baptizing, if you're not the Christ, or Elijah, or the prophet?" John hit 'em with, "I'm baptizing with water, but there's somebody standing right in your crowd that you don't even know about. He's the one coming after me, but he's so far ahead of me it's not even close -- I'm not even worthy to untie his sandal strap, no lie." All this went down in Bethany, past the Jordan, where John was out there dunking folks.

web_text: The next day, he saw Jesus coming to him, and said, “Behold, the Lamb of God, who takes away the sin of the world! This is he of whom I said, ‘After me comes a man who is preferred before me, for he was before me.’ I didn’t know him, but for this reason I came baptizing in water: that he would be revealed to Israel.” John testified, saying, “I have seen the Spirit descending like a dove out of heaven, and it remained on him.
slang_text: Next day, John spots Jesus coming his way and says, "Get a load of this -- the Lamb of God, the one hauling away the sin of the whole world! This is the guy I was telling you about, 'The one coming after me is already way ahead of me, 'cause he existed before me.' I didn't have a clue who he was, but that's exactly why I showed up baptizing with water -- so he'd get revealed to Israel." Then John laid it out, "I saw the Spirit float down like a dove outta the sky, and it stuck right there on him, fer sure."

web_text: I didn’t recognize him, but he who sent me to baptize in water, he said to me, ‘On whomever you will see the Spirit descending, and remaining on him, the same is he who baptizes in the Holy Spirit.’ I have seen, and have testified that this is the Son of God.” Again, the next day, John was standing with two of his disciples, and he looked at Jesus as he walked, and said, “Behold, the Lamb of God!”
slang_text: "I didn't have a clue who he was at first, but the one who sent me to baptize with water told me, 'Whoever you see the Spirit land on and stick to -- that's the one who baptizes with the Holy Spirit.' And I saw it go down with my own eyes, and I'm telling you fer sure, this is the Son of God." Next day, John's posted up with two of his disciples, and he catches sight of Jesus walking by and says, "Get a load of that -- the Lamb of God!"

web_text:
The two disciples heard him speak, and they followed Jesus. Jesus turned, and saw them following, and said to them,
“What are you looking for?”
They said to him, “Rabbi” (which is to say, being interpreted, Teacher), “where are you staying?” He said to them,
“Come, and see.”
They came and saw where he was staying, and they stayed with him that day. It was about the tenth hour. One of the two who heard John, and followed him, was Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother.
slang_text: The two disciples heard him say that and bounced after Jesus. Jesus spins around, spots them tagging along, and says, "What are you dudes after?" They said, "Rabbi" (which just means Teacher), "where you crashing at?" He told them, "Come on, check it out." So they went, saw where he was staying, and hung out with him the rest of the day -- musta been about the tenth hour. One of the two who heard John and took off after Jesus was Andrew, Simon Peter's brother.

web_text:
He first found his own brother, Simon, and said to him, “We have found the Messiah!” (which is, being interpreted, Christ). He brought him to Jesus. Jesus looked at him, and said,
“You are Simon the son of Jonah. You shall be called Cephas” (which is by interpretation, Peter). On the next day, he was determined to go out into Galilee, and he found Philip. Jesus said to him,
“Follow me.” Now Philip was from Bethsaida, of the city of Andrew and Peter.
slang_text: First thing Andrew does is track down his own brother Simon and tell him, "We found the Messiah!" (that's Christ, for the translation-impaired). He hauled him straight over to Jesus. Jesus looks him up and down and says, "You're Simon, son of Jonah. From here on out you're gonna be called Cephas" (that's Peter). Next day, Jesus made up his mind to head out to Galilee, and he tracked down Philip. Jesus told him, "Follow me." Now Philip was from Bethsaida, same turf as Andrew and Peter.

web_text:
Philip found Nathanael, and said to him, “We have found him, of whom Moses in the law, and the prophets, wrote: Jesus of Nazareth, the son of Joseph.” Nathanael said to him, “Can any good thing come out of Nazareth?”
Philip said to him, “Come and see.” Jesus saw Nathanael coming to him, and said about him,
“Behold, an Israelite indeed, in whom is no deceit!” Nathanael said to him, “How do you know me?”
Jesus answered him,
“Before Philip called you, when you were under the fig tree, I saw you.”
slang_text: Philip finds Nathanael and tells him, "We found him -- the one Moses wrote about in the law, the one the prophets talked about: Jesus of Nazareth, Joseph's kid." Nathanael goes, "Nazareth? Barf me out, can anything decent even come outta that dump?" Philip just says, "Come see for yourself, dipstick." Jesus spots Nathanael walking up and says about him, "Now there's a righteous Israelite right there, not a shred of deceit in him!" Nathanael says, "How do you even know me?" Jesus answers, "Before Philip even called you, when you were chillin' under that fig tree, I already clocked you."

web_text:
Nathanael answered him, “Rabbi, you are the Son of God! You are King of Israel!” Jesus answered him,
“Because I told you, ‘I saw you underneath the fig tree,’ do you believe? You will see greater things than these!” He said to him,
“Most certainly, I tell you all, hereafter you will see heaven opened, and the angels of God ascending and descending on the Son of Man.”
slang_text: Nathanael comes back with, "Rabbi, you're the Son of God! You're the King of Israel!" Jesus answers him, "You believe just 'cause I said I saw you under the fig tree? Buckle up, you're gonna see way more epic stuff than that!" Then he tells him, "No duh, I'm telling all of you -- from here on out you're gonna see heaven cracked wide open, and God's angels cruising up and down on the Son of Man."

web_text:
The third day, there was a marriage in Cana of Galilee. Jesus’ mother was there. Jesus also was invited, with his disciples, to the marriage. When the wine ran out, Jesus’ mother said to him, “They have no wine.” Jesus said to her,
“Woman, what does that have to do with you and me? My hour has not yet come.”
slang_text: Third day in, there's a totally bodacious wedding happening in Cana, up in Galilee. Jesus' mom was there, and Jesus and his posse got invited too. When the wine ran out -- way harsh, dude -- his mom comes over and says, "They're outta wine." Jesus goes, "Ma, what's that got to do with you and me? My hour hasn't come yet, so don't have a cow."

web_text:
His mother said to the servants, “Whatever he says to you, do it.” Now there were six water pots of stone set there after the Jews’ way of purifying, containing two or three metretes apiece. Jesus said to them,
“Fill the water pots with water.” They filled them up to the brim. He said to them,
“Now draw some out, and take it to the ruler of the feast.” So they took it.
slang_text: His mom turns to the servants, no joke, and says, "Whatever he tells you, just do it, fer sure." Now there were six massive stone jars parked there for the Jewish purification thing, each one holding like 20-30 gallons. Jesus tells the servants, "Fill these jars up with water." So they filled 'em clear to the brim, like a boss. Then he goes, "Now dip some out and haul it over to whoever's running this shindig." So they did.

web_text: When the ruler of the feast tasted the water now become wine, and didn’t know where it came from (but the servants who had drawn the water knew), the ruler of the feast called the bridegroom, and said to him, “Everyone serves the good wine first, and when the guests have drunk freely, then that which is worse. You have kept the good wine until now!” This beginning of his signs Jesus did in Cana of Galilee, and revealed his glory; and his disciples believed in him. After this, he went down to Capernaum, he, and his mother, his brothers, and his disciples; and they stayed there a few days.
slang_text: So the party host takes a sip of the water -- which is now, no lie, straight-up wine -- and he's totally clueless where it came from (though the servants who dipped it knew the whole deal). The host waves over the groom and says, "Everybody pours the primo stuff first, and once folks have had a few rounds, that's when you sneak out the bogus cheap stuff. But you? You kept the choice wine till now!" This was the first righteous sign Jesus busted out, right there in Cana of Galilee, and it showed everybody his glory -- his disciples believed in him for real after that. After this, he headed down to Capernaum with his mom, his brothers, and his disciples, and they hung out there a few days.

web_text:
The Passover of the Jews was at hand, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem. He found in the temple those who sold oxen, sheep, and doves, and the changers of money sitting. He made a whip of cords, and threw all out of the temple, both the sheep and the oxen; and he poured out the changers’ money, and overthrew their tables. To those who sold the doves, he said,
“Take these things out of here! Don’t make my Father’s house a marketplace!”
slang_text: The Jewish Passover was almost here, so Jesus bounced up to Jerusalem. He walks into the temple and finds people slinging cattle, sheep, and doves, plus money-changers parked there doing their grody business. Talk about bogus. He braids himself a whip out of cords and clears everybody out -- sheep, cattle, the whole scene -- dumps the money-changers' cash and flips their tables like a spaz. To the dove sellers he says, "Haul this junk outta here! Don't you dare turn my Father's house into some marketplace!"

web_text:
His disciples remembered that it was written, “Zeal for your house will eat me up.” The Jews therefore answered him, “What sign do you show us, seeing that you do these things?” Jesus answered them,
“Destroy this temple, and in three days I will raise it up.” The Jews therefore said, “It took forty-six years to build this temple! Will you raise it up in three days?”
slang_text: His disciples remembered it was written somewhere, "My zeal for your house is gonna totally consume me." So the Jews come back at him with, "What's your damage -- what sign you got to show for pulling a stunt like this?" Jesus answers, "Wreck this temple, and in three days I'll raise it right back up." The Jews were like, "As if! This temple took forty-six years to build, dude! You're gonna rebuild it in three days?"

web_text: But he spoke of the temple of his body. When therefore he was raised from the dead, his disciples remembered that he said this, and they believed the Scripture, and the word which Jesus had said. Now when he was in Jerusalem at the Passover, during the feast, many believed in his name, observing his signs which he did. But Jesus didn’t entrust himself to them, because he knew everyone,
slang_text: But he was actually talking about the temple of his own bod. So when he got raised from the dead, his disciples remembered he'd said that, and they believed the Scripture, fer sure, and what Jesus had told them. Now while he was in Jerusalem for Passover, during the whole feast, a bunch of people believed in his name after checking out the epic signs he was pulling off. But Jesus wasn't handing himself over to any of them, 'cause he had everybody's number --

web_text: and because he didn’t need for anyone to testify concerning man; for he himself knew what was in man.
slang_text: -- didn't need anybody to clue him in on people, 'cause no duh, he already knew exactly what was going on inside 'em.

web_text:
Now there was a man of the Pharisees named Nicodemus, a ruler of the Jews. The same came to him by night, and said to him, “Rabbi, we know that you are a teacher come from God, for no one can do these signs that you do, unless God is with him.” Jesus answered him,
“Most certainly, I tell you, unless one is born anew,

he can’t see God’s Kingdom.” Nicodemus said to him, “How can a man be born when he is old? Can he enter a second time into his mother’s womb, and be born?”
slang_text: So there's this Pharisee named Nicodemus, a righteous big shot among the Jews. Dude sneaks over to Jesus at night and goes, "Rabbi, we know you're a teacher straight from God -- no way anybody pulls off the signs you do unless God's backing him up." Jesus tells him, "For real, unless a person gets born all over again, they can't even see God's Kingdom." Nicodemus is trippin', like, "Say what? How's a grown man supposed to get born again? He can't exactly climb back into his mom and pop out twice."

web_text:
Jesus answered,
“Most certainly I tell you, unless one is born of water and spirit, he can’t enter into God’s Kingdom. That which is born of the flesh is flesh. That which is born of the Spirit is spirit. Don’t marvel that I said to you, ‘You must be born anew.’ The wind
blows where it wants to, and you hear its sound, but don’t know where it comes from and where it is going. So is everyone who is born of the Spirit.”
slang_text: Jesus comes back with, "No lie, unless somebody's born of water and spirit, they ain't gettin' into God's Kingdom. Whatever's born from flesh is just flesh, plain and simple. Whatever's born from the Spirit is spirit. Don't get all bent outta shape 'cause I said, 'You gotta be born again.' The wind blows wherever it feels like blowin' -- you hear it whistlin' through, but you can't tell where it came from or where it's bouncin' to. Same deal with everybody born of the Spirit."

web_text:
Nicodemus answered him, “How can these things be?” Jesus answered him,
“Are you the teacher of Israel, and don’t understand these things? Most certainly I tell you, we speak that which we know, and testify of that which we have seen, and you don’t receive our witness. If I told you earthly things and you don’t believe, how will you believe if I tell you heavenly things?
slang_text: Nicodemus goes, "How's any of this even bogus -- I mean, possible?" Jesus fires back, "You're supposed to be Israel's big teacher and you don't get a clue? Straight up, we're talkin' about stuff we actually know, testifyin' to what we've actually seen with our own eyes, and y'all are still like, 'as if.' If I tell you about earthly stuff and that's already too much for you, how you gonna handle it when I lay heavenly stuff on you?

web_text: No one has ascended into heaven, but he who descended out of heaven, the Son of Man, who is in heaven. As Moses lifted up the serpent in the wilderness, even so must the Son of Man be lifted up, that whoever believes in him should not perish, but have eternal life. For God so loved the world, that he gave his one and only Son, that whoever believes in him should not perish, but have eternal life.
slang_text: Nobody's ever bounced up to heaven 'cept the one who came down from heaven -- the Son of Man, who's still up there. Just like Moses stuck that snake up on a pole out in the wilderness, the Son of Man's gotta get lifted up the same righteous way, so whoever puts their faith in him doesn't wipe out, but scores life that never quits. Check it -- God loved the world so hard, so totally, that he handed over his one and only Son, so whoever believes in him doesn't wipe out, but scores life that never quits.

web_text: For God didn’t send his Son into the world to judge the world, but that the world should be saved through him. He who believes in him is not judged. He who doesn’t believe has been judged already, because he has not believed in the name of the one and only Son of God. This is the judgment, that the light has come into the world, and men loved the darkness rather than the light; for their works were evil. For everyone who does evil hates the light, and doesn’t come to the light, lest his works would be exposed.
slang_text: Check it, God didn't send his Son into the world to throw the book at everybody -- he sent him so the whole world could get saved through him. Whoever believes in him don't get judged, no duh. Whoever doesn't believe already stands judged, 'cause they never believed in the name of God's one and only Son. And here's the verdict: the light showed up in the world, and people loved the darkness more than the light, 'cause what they were pullin' was grody to the max. Everybody who does dirt hates the light and won't come near it, so their bogus deeds don't get busted out in the open.

web_text: But he who does the truth comes to the light, that his works may be revealed, that they have been done in God.” After these things, Jesus came with his disciples into the land of Judea. He stayed there with them, and baptized. John also was baptizing in Enon near Salim, because there was much water there. They came, and were baptized. For John was not yet thrown into prison.
slang_text: But whoever's rollin' with the truth comes to the light, so it's plain their stuff was done through God." After all that, Jesus bounced out to Judea with his disciples. He hung out there with them, baptizing folks. John was also out baptizing over in Enon, near Salim, 'cause the water was flowin' plentiful out there. People kept showin' up to get dunked -- this was before John got tossed in the slammer.

web_text: There arose therefore a questioning on the part of John’s disciples with some Jews about purification. They came to John, and said to him, “Rabbi, he who was with you beyond the Jordan, to whom you have testified, behold, the same baptizes, and everyone is coming to him.” John answered, “A man can receive nothing, unless it has been given him from heaven. You yourselves testify that I said, ‘I am not the Christ,’ but, ‘I have been sent before him.’
slang_text: So John's disciples started beefin' with some Jews about purification stuff. They came up to John and said, "Rabbi, remember that guy who was with you past the Jordan, the one you vouched for? Get this -- he's baptizing people now, and everybody's flocking to him." John answers, "Nobody scores anything unless it gets handed to 'em from heaven. Y'all can testify I said, 'I'm not the Christ,' just, 'I got sent on ahead of him.'

web_text: He who has the bride is the bridegroom; but the friend of the bridegroom, who stands and hears him, rejoices greatly because of the bridegroom’s voice. This, my joy, therefore is made full. He must increase, but I must decrease. He who comes from above is above all. He who is from the earth belongs to the earth, and speaks of the earth. He who comes from heaven is above all. What he has seen and heard, of that he testifies; and no one receives his witness.
slang_text: Whoever's got the bride is the groom -- but the groom's main man, the one just standin' there listenin' in, gets stoked just hearin' the groom's voice. So my joy's totally tubular right about now. He's gotta get bigger, and I gotta fade out. Whoever comes from above is above everybody, fer sure. Whoever's from earth belongs to earth and talks earth talk. Whoever comes from heaven is above the whole scene. He testifies to what he's actually seen and heard, and nobody's buyin' it.

web_text:
He who has received his witness has set his seal to this, that God is true. For he whom God has sent speaks the words of God; for God gives the Spirit without measure. The Father loves the Son, and has given all things into his hand. One who believes in the Son has eternal life, but one who disobeys
 the Son won’t see life, but the wrath of God remains on him.”
slang_text: Whoever's accepted his witness is puttin' their stamp on it, sayin' God's the real deal. 'Cause the one God sent speaks God's own words -- God hands him the Spirit with no limits, none. The Father's got mad love for the Son and put everything in his hands. Whoever believes in the Son has got life that never runs dry, but whoever won't get with the program on the Son -- forget it, no life for him, just God's wrath stickin' to him like glue."

web_text: Therefore when the Lord knew that the Pharisees had heard that Jesus was making and baptizing more disciples than John (although Jesus himself didn’t baptize, but his disciples), he left Judea, and departed into Galilee. He needed to pass through Samaria.
slang_text: So when Jesus caught wind the Pharisees heard he was making and dunking more disciples than John (though it was really his crew doing the dunking, not him), he bounced outta Judea and pointed himself toward Galilee. He had to cut through Samaria to get there.

web_text:
So he came to a city of Samaria, called Sychar, near the parcel of ground that Jacob gave to his son, Joseph. Jacob’s well was there. Jesus therefore, being tired from his journey, sat down by the well. It was about the sixth hour. A woman of Samaria came to draw water. Jesus said to her,
“Give me a drink.” For his disciples had gone away into the city to buy food.
slang_text:
So he rolled into this Samaria town called Sychar, right by the land Jacob left to his kid Joseph. Jacob's well was sitting right there. Jesus was wiped out from the road, so he chilled by the well. It was about noon. A Samaritan Betty came out to fetch water. Jesus goes,
"Slide me a drink." His disciples had bounced into town to scrounge up some grub.

web_text:
The Samaritan woman therefore said to him, “How is it that you, being a Jew, ask for a drink from me, a Samaritan woman?” (For Jews have no dealings with Samaritans.) Jesus answered her,
“If you knew the gift of God, and who it is who says to you, ‘Give me a drink,’ you would have asked him, and he would have given you living water.” The woman said to him, “Sir, you have nothing to draw with, and the well is deep. So where do you get that living water? Are you greater than our father, Jacob, who gave us the well, and drank of it himself, as did his children, and his livestock?”
slang_text:
So the Samaritan woman's like, "As if -- you're a Jew, and you're asking me, a Samaritan dudette, for a drink?" (See, Jews and Samaritans flat-out didn't mess with each other back then.) Jesus comes back with,
"If you knew what God's got to give, and who's asking you for a drink right now, you'd be the one asking him -- and he'd toss you living water." The woman goes, "Uh, mister, you ain't even got a bucket, and this well's gnarly deep. So where's this 'living water' supposed to come from? You think you're bigger than our ancestor Jacob, who gave us this well and drank from it himself, along with his kids and his livestock?"

web_text:
Jesus answered her,
“Everyone who drinks of this water will thirst again, but whoever drinks of the water that I will give him will never thirst again; but the water that I will give him will become in him a well of water springing up to eternal life.” The woman said to him, “Sir, give me this water, so that I don’t get thirsty, neither come all the way here to draw.” Jesus said to her,
“Go, call your husband, and come here.”
slang_text:
Jesus fired back,
"Anybody who drinks this water's gonna get thirsty again, no duh. But whoever drinks the water I dish out never gets thirsty again -- that's a promise. That water's gonna turn into a spring inside 'em, bubbling up into life that never quits." The woman goes, "Okay mister, gimme that water so I quit getting thirsty and quit dragging myself out here to fill buckets." Jesus tells her,
"Go get your husband and bring him here."

web_text:
The woman answered, “I have no husband.”
Jesus said to her,
“You said well, ‘I have no husband,’ for you have had five husbands; and he whom you now have is not your husband. This you have said truly.” The woman said to him, “Sir, I perceive that you are a prophet. Our fathers worshiped in this mountain, and you Jews say that in Jerusalem is the place where people ought to worship.”
slang_text:
The woman says, "I ain't got a husband."
Jesus goes,
"You said it right -- 'I ain't got a husband.' 'Cause you've been through five husbands, and the dude you're with now ain't even your husband. You told it straight on that one." The woman goes, "Whoa, mister, I can tell you're a prophet. Now, our ancestors worshiped up on this mountain, but you Jews keep saying Jerusalem's the spot where folks gotta worship."

web_text:
Jesus said to her,
“Woman, believe me, the hour comes, when neither in this mountain, nor in Jerusalem, will you worship the Father. You worship that which you don’t know. We worship that which we know; for salvation is from the Jews. But the hour comes, and now is, when the true worshipers will worship the Father in spirit and truth, for the Father seeks such to be his worshipers. God is spirit, and those who worship him must worship in spirit and truth.”
slang_text:
Jesus tells her,
"Listen up, lady -- a time's coming when it won't be about this mountain or Jerusalem for worshiping the Father. You Samaritans worship what you don't even know, but we worship what we know, 'cause salvation comes outta the Jews. But get this -- the time's coming, and it's basically already here -- when the real worshipers are gonna worship the Father in spirit and truth. That's exactly what the Father's after, worshipers like that. God is spirit, so whoever worships him's gotta do it in spirit and truth."

Chill here in me, and I'll chill in you. 'Cause a branch can't do a bodacious thing on its own unless it's rooted in the vine -- same deal for you, unless you remain in me.

web_text: I am the vine. You are the branches. He who remains in me, and I in him, the same bears much fruit, for apart from me you can do nothing. If a man doesn’t remain in me, he is thrown out as a branch, and is withered; and they gather them, throw them into the fire, and they are burned. If you remain in me, and my words remain in you, you will ask whatever you desire, and it will be done for you. “In this is my Father glorified, that you bear much fruit; and so you will be my disciples.
slang_text: I'm the vine, you're the branches, no duh. Whoever chills in me, and I'm chilling in them, that person cranks out bodacious fruit -- 'cause without me you got zip, zilch, nada. If some dipstick doesn't stay with me, he gets tossed out like a dead branch and withers up, and they rake those up, chuck 'em in the fire, and that's the end of the story. But if you stay with me, and my words stay in you, ask for whatever you want and it's yours, fer sure. "This is what makes my Father look totally righteous -- you guys cranking out epic fruit, and that's how the world knows you're really my disciples.

web_text: Even as the Father has loved me, I also have loved you. Remain in my love. If you keep my commandments, you will remain in my love; even as I have kept my Father’s commandments, and remain in his love. I have spoken these things to you, that my joy may remain in you, and that your joy may be made full. “This is my commandment, that you love one another, even as I have loved you.
slang_text: Just like the Father's loved me, I've loved you guys the exact same way, for real. Stay chill in my love. If you keep my commands, you'll stay in my love, same as I've kept my Father's commands and stayed in his love. I'm telling you all this so my joy sticks with you like glue, and your joy gets cranked all the way up. "Here's my command, dude: love each other, same way I've loved you.

web_text: Greater love has no one than this, that someone lay down his life for his friends. You are my friends, if you do whatever I command you. No longer do I call you servants, for the servant doesn’t know what his lord does. But I have called you friends, for everything that I heard from my Father, I have made known to you. You didn’t choose me, but I chose you, and appointed you, that you should go and bear fruit, and that your fruit should remain; that whatever you will ask of the Father in my name, he may give it to you.
slang_text: There's no more righteous love than this: somebody laying down their life for their crew. You guys are my friends, fer sure, as long as you do what I'm telling you. I'm not calling you servants anymore, 'cause a servant doesn't have a clue what his boss is up to. But I've called you friends, 'cause everything I heard from my Father, I laid it all out for you, no holding back. You didn't pick me -- I picked you, straight up, and set you up to go out and bear fruit, fruit that sticks around for the long haul, so that whatever you ask the Father in my name, he'll hook you up, no doubt.

web_text:
“I command these things to you, that you may love one another. If the world hates you, you know that it has hated me before it hated you. If you were of the world, the world would love its own. But because you are not of the world, since I chose you out of the world, therefore the world hates you. Remember the word that I said to you: ‘A servant is not greater than his lord.’
If they persecuted me, they will also persecute you. If they kept my word, they will also keep yours.
slang_text:
"I'm laying these commands on you so you'll love each other. If the world's giving you grief, just know it was hating on me way before it started trippin' on you. If you were part of the world's scene, the world would be all about its own crew. But since you're not part of that scene -- 'cause I plucked you outta it -- that's exactly why the world's coming at you like a bad scene. Remember what I told you: 'A servant's not bigger than his boss.'
If they came after me, they're gonna come after you too. If they kept my word, they'll keep yours too.

‘A little while, and you won’t see me, and again a little while, and you will see me;’ and,
‘Because I go to the Father’?” They said therefore, “What is this that he says,

Now Judas, who betrayed him, also knew the place, for Jesus often met there with his disciples. Judas then, having taken a detachment of soldiers and officers from the chief priests and the Pharisees, came there with lanterns, torches, and weapons. Jesus therefore, knowing all the things that were happening to him, went out, and said to them,
“Who are you looking for?”

Pilate therefore went out to them, and said, “What accusation do you bring against this man?” They answered him, “If this man weren’t an evildoer, we wouldn’t have delivered him up to you.” Pilate therefore said to them, “Take him yourselves, and judge him according to your law.”
Therefore the Jews said to him, “It is not lawful for us to put anyone to death,” that the word of Jesus might be fulfilled, which he spoke, signifying by what kind of death he should die.
slang_text:

“My Kingdom is not of this world. If my Kingdom were of this world, then my servants would fight, that I wouldn’t be delivered to the Jews. But now my Kingdom is not from here.”
slang_text:
So Pilate went back inside the Praetorium, called Jesus over, and said, "You the King of the Jews, or what?" Jesus answered him,
"That your own idea, or did somebody feed you that about me?" Pilate answered, "I'm no Jew, dude. Your own people and the chief priests handed you over to me. What'd you even do?" Jesus answered,
"My Kingdom ain't of this world. If it were, my people would be out there throwing down so I wouldn't get handed over to the Jews. But like I said -- my Kingdom's not from around here."

web_text:
Pilate therefore said to him, “Are you a king then?”
Jesus answered,
“You say that I am a king. For this reason I have been born, and for this reason I have come into the world, that I should testify to the truth. Everyone who is of the truth listens to my voice.” Pilate said to him, “What is truth?”
When he had said this, he went out again to the Jews, and said to them, “I find no basis for a charge against him. But you have a custom, that I should release someone to you at the Passover. Therefore, do you want me to release to you the King of the Jews?” Then they all shouted again, saying, “Not this man, but Barabbas!” Now Barabbas was a robber.
slang_text:
So Pilate said to him, "Oh, so you ARE a king, then?"
Jesus answered,
"That's your word, not mine. This is exactly why I was born, and exactly why I came into this world -- to testify to the truth. Everybody who's on the side of truth listens to my voice." Pilate goes, "Truth? Like, what even is that?"
And after saying that, he went back out to the Jews and told them, "I got nothing on this guy -- no basis for any charge. But hey, y'all got that custom where I spring somebody at Passover. So you want me to cut the King of the Jews loose?" Then the whole crowd started shouting, "Not him -- Barabbas!" And Barabbas, by the way, was a straight-up robber.

web_text: So Pilate then took Jesus, and flogged him. The soldiers twisted thorns into a crown, and put it on his head, and dressed him in a purple garment. They kept saying, “Hail, King of the Jews!” and they kept slapping him. Then Pilate went out again, and said to them, “Behold, I bring him out to you, that you may know that I find no basis for a charge against him.”
slang_text: So Pilate takes Jesus off and has him flogged -- brutal, for real. The soldiers get grody with it, twisting thorns into a crown and jamming it down on his head, then throwing a purple robe over him. They keep coming right up in his face, saying, "Hail, King of the Jews!" and slapping him again and again. Then Pilate comes back out and says, "Check it -- I'm bringing him out here so you'll know I find nothing to charge him with."

web_text:
Jesus therefore came out, wearing the crown of thorns and the purple garment. Pilate said to them, “Behold, the man!” When therefore the chief priests and the officers saw him, they shouted, saying, “Crucify! Crucify!”
Pilate said to them, “Take him yourselves, and crucify him, for I find no basis for a charge against him.” The Jews answered him, “We have a law, and by our law he ought to die, because he made himself the Son of God.” When therefore Pilate heard this saying, he was more afraid.
slang_text:
So Jesus comes out wearing the thorn crown and the purple robe. Pilate says, "Behold -- here's the man!" But soon as the chief priests and the guards catch sight of him, they start shouting, "Crucify him! Crucify him!"
Pilate tells them, "Take him yourselves and crucify him, 'cause I find nothing to charge him with." The Jews answer, "We've got a law, and by that law he's gotta die, 'cause he made himself out to be the Son of God." When Pilate heard that, he got even more spooked.

web_text:
He entered into the Praetorium again, and said to Jesus, “Where are you from?” But Jesus gave him no answer. Pilate therefore said to him, “Aren’t you speaking to me? Don’t you know that I have power to release you, and have power to crucify you?” Jesus answered,
“You would have no power at all against me, unless it were given to you from above. Therefore he who delivered me to you has greater sin.” At this, Pilate was seeking to release him, but the Jews cried out, saying, “If you release this man, you aren’t Caesar’s friend! Everyone who makes himself a king speaks against Caesar!”
slang_text:
He heads back into the Praetorium and asks Jesus, "Where are you even from?" But Jesus doesn't answer him. So Pilate says, "You gonna stay quiet on me? Don't you know I've got the power to release you, and the power to crucify you?" Jesus answers,
"You wouldn't have any power over me at all if it wasn't handed down to you from above. That's why the one who handed me over to you carries the greater sin." After that, Pilate's looking for a way to release him, but the Jews cry out, "You let this man go, you're no friend of Caesar's! Anybody who makes himself a king is speaking against Caesar!"

web_text:
When Pilate therefore heard these words, he brought Jesus out, and sat down on the judgment seat at a place called “The Pavement”, but in Hebrew, “Gabbatha.” Now it was the Preparation Day of the Passover, at about the sixth hour. He said to the Jews, “Behold, your King!” They cried out, “Away with him! Away with him! Crucify him!”
Pilate said to them, “Shall I crucify your King?”
The chief priests answered, “We have no king but Caesar!” So then he delivered him to them to be crucified. So they took Jesus and led him away.
slang_text:
When Pilate hears that, he brings Jesus back out and takes his seat on the judgment seat, at a spot called "The Pavement" -- "Gabbatha" in Hebrew. It's the Preparation Day of the Passover, around noon. He tells the Jews, "Behold, your King!" They cry out, "Get him outta here! Get him outta here! Crucify him!"
Pilate asks, "You really want me to crucify your King?"
The chief priests answer, "We've got no king but Caesar!" So he hands Jesus over to be crucified. And they took Jesus and led him away.

web_text: He went out, bearing his cross, to the place called “The Place of a Skull”, which is called in Hebrew, “Golgotha”, where they crucified him, and with him two others, on either side one, and Jesus in the middle. Pilate wrote a title also, and put it on the cross. There was written, “JESUS OF NAZARETH, THE KING OF THE JEWS.” Therefore many of the Jews read this title, for the place where Jesus was crucified was near the city; and it was written in Hebrew, in Latin, and in Greek.
slang_text: He goes out carrying his own cross, to a place called "The Place of a Skull" -- "Golgotha" in Hebrew -- and that's where they crucify him, along with two others, one on each side, Jesus in the middle. Pilate also has a sign written up and fixed to the cross. It read, "JESUS OF NAZARETH, THE KING OF THE JEWS." A lot of the Jews read that sign, 'cause the place where Jesus was crucified was close to the city, and it was written in Hebrew, Latin, and Greek.

web_text:
The chief priests of the Jews therefore said to Pilate, “Don’t write, ‘The King of the Jews,’ but, ‘he said, “I am King of the Jews.”’” Pilate answered, “What I have written, I have written.” Then the soldiers, when they had crucified Jesus, took his garments and made four parts, to every soldier a part; and also the coat. Now the coat was without seam, woven from the top throughout. Then they said to one another, “Let’s not tear it, but cast lots for it to decide whose it will be,” that the Scripture might be fulfilled, which says,
“They parted my garments among them.
For my cloak they cast lots.”
Therefore the soldiers did these things.
slang_text:
So the chief priests go to Pilate and say, "Don't write 'The King of the Jews' -- write that he said, 'I am King of the Jews.'" Pilate answers, "What I've written, I've written." Then the soldiers, once they'd crucified Jesus, take his clothes and split them four ways, one share for each soldier, plus the coat. That coat had no seam, woven top to bottom in one piece. So they say to each other, "Let's not tear it up -- let's cast lots for it and see who gets it," so the Scripture would come true, the one that says,
"They divided my garments among them.
And for my clothing they cast lots."
So that's exactly what the soldiers did.

web_text:
But there were standing by the cross of Jesus his mother, and his mother’s sister, Mary the wife of Clopas, and Mary Magdalene. Therefore when Jesus saw his mother, and the disciple whom he loved standing there, he said to his mother,
“Woman, behold, your son!” Then he said to the disciple,
“Behold, your mother!” From that hour, the disciple took her to his own home. After this, Jesus, seeing that all things were now finished, that the Scripture might be fulfilled, said,
“I am thirsty.”
slang_text:
Standing right by Jesus's cross were his mother, her sister, Mary the wife of Clopas, and Mary Magdalene. So when Jesus sees his mother, and the disciple he loved standing there, he tells his mother,
"Woman, behold your son!" Then he tells the disciple,
"Behold your mother!" And from that hour on, the disciple took her into his own home. After this, Jesus, knowing everything was now finished so the Scripture would come true, says,
"I'm thirsty."

web_text:
Now a vessel full of vinegar was set there; so they put a sponge full of the vinegar on hyssop, and held it at his mouth. When Jesus therefore had received the vinegar, he said,
“It is finished.” He bowed his head, and gave up his spirit. Therefore the Jews, because it was the Preparation Day, so that the bodies wouldn’t remain on the cross on the Sabbath (for that Sabbath was a special one), asked of Pilate that their legs might be broken, and that they might be taken away. Therefore the soldiers came, and broke the legs of the first, and of the other who was crucified with him;
slang_text:
There was a jar of vinegar sitting there, so they soak a sponge in it, fix it on a hyssop branch, and hold it up to his mouth. As soon as Jesus takes the vinegar, he says,
"It is finished." Then he bows his head and gives up his spirit. Now the Jews, since it was Preparation Day, didn't want the bodies left hanging on the crosses through the Sabbath (and this Sabbath was an especially important one), so they asked Pilate to have the legs broken and the bodies taken down. So the soldiers come and break the legs of the first man, and of the other one crucified with him;

web_text: but when they came to Jesus, and saw that he was already dead, they didn’t break his legs. However one of the soldiers pierced his side with a spear, and immediately blood and water came out. He who has seen has testified, and his testimony is true. He knows that he tells the truth, that you may believe. For these things happened, that the Scripture might be fulfilled, “A bone of him will not be broken.”
slang_text: but when they reach Jesus and see he's already dead, they don't bother breaking his legs. Instead, one of the soldiers drives a spear into his side, and right away blood and water come out. The one who saw it has testified, and what he says is true. He knows he's telling the truth, so that you may believe. All this happened so the Scripture would come true, the one that says, "Not one of his bones will be broken."

web_text: Again another Scripture says, “They will look on him whom they pierced.” After these things, Joseph of Arimathaea, being a disciple of Jesus, but secretly for fear of the Jews, asked of Pilate that he might take away Jesus’ body. Pilate gave him permission. He came therefore and took away his body. Nicodemus, who at first came to Jesus by night, also came bringing a mixture of myrrh and aloes, about a hundred Roman pounds. So they took Jesus’ body, and bound it in linen cloths with the spices, as the custom of the Jews is to bury.
slang_text: And another Scripture says, "They will look on the one they pierced." After all this, Joseph of Arimathaea -- a disciple of Jesus, though keeping it quiet 'cause he was afraid of the Jews -- asks Pilate for permission to take Jesus's body away. Pilate grants it. So Joseph comes and takes the body. Nicodemus, the same one who'd come to Jesus by night before, comes too, bringing a mixture of myrrh and aloes, about a hundred Roman pounds' worth. So they take Jesus's body and wrap it in linen cloths with the spices, just as the Jews' custom for burial goes.

web_text: Now in the place where he was crucified there was a garden. In the garden was a new tomb in which no man had ever yet been laid. Then because of the Jews’ Preparation Day (for the tomb was near at hand) they laid Jesus there.
slang_text: Now in the place where he was crucified there was a garden, and in that garden a brand-new tomb, one nobody had ever been laid in. So because it was the Jews' Preparation Day, and the tomb was close by, they laid Jesus there.

web_text: Now on the first day of the week, Mary Magdalene went early, while it was still dark, to the tomb, and saw the stone taken away from the tomb. Therefore she ran and came to Simon Peter, and to the other disciple whom Jesus loved, and said to them, “They have taken away the Lord out of the tomb, and we don’t know where they have laid him!” Therefore Peter and the other disciple went out, and they went toward the tomb. They both ran together. The other disciple outran Peter, and came to the tomb first.
slang_text: So it's crack of dawn Sunday, still dark as midnight, and Mary Magdalene bounces on over to the tomb -- and oh snap, the stone's rolled clean away. She takes off running, no joke, to track down Simon Peter and that other disciple, the one Jesus was totally tight with, and blurts, "They hauled the Lord outta the tomb, dudes, and we got zero clue where they stashed him!" So Peter and the other guy bounce for the tomb, both hauling tail together. The other disciple straight-up smokes Peter in the race and gets there first, like a boss.

web_text: Stooping and looking in, he saw the linen cloths lying, yet he didn’t enter in. Then Simon Peter came, following him, and entered into the tomb. He saw the linen cloths lying, and the cloth that had been on his head, not lying with the linen cloths, but rolled up in a place by itself. So then the other disciple who came first to the tomb also entered in, and he saw and believed.
slang_text: He crouches down, peeks in, and spots the linen wraps just chillin' there, but he doesn't go in, no way. Then Simon Peter rolls up right behind him and marches straight into the tomb, zero hesitation. He clocks the linen wraps lying there, plus the cloth that covered Jesus's head -- not tossed with the rest, but folded up neat and tidy, off on its own. Then the other disciple, the one who got there first, finally ducks in too -- and he sees it and believes, fer sure.

web_text: For as yet they didn’t know the Scripture, that he must rise from the dead. So the disciples went away again to their own homes. But Mary was standing outside at the tomb weeping. So, as she wept, she stooped and looked into the tomb, and she saw two angels in white sitting, one at the head, and one at the feet, where the body of Jesus had lain.
slang_text: Thing is, they still hadn't gotten a clue that the Scripture said he had to rise from the dead -- no duh they were lost. So the disciples just bounce back home. But Mary's outside the tomb, bawling her eyes out something fierce. And while she's crying, she stoops down and peeks into the tomb, and check it -- two angels in white just sitting there, one where his head used to be and one where his feet used to be.

web_text:
They told her, “Woman, why are you weeping?”
She said to them, “Because they have taken away my Lord, and I don’t know where they have laid him.” When she had said this, she turned around and saw Jesus standing, and didn’t know that it was Jesus. Jesus said to her,
“Woman, why are you weeping? Who are you looking for?”
She, supposing him to be the gardener, said to him, “Sir, if you have carried him away, tell me where you have laid him, and I will take him away.” Jesus said to her,
“Mary.”
She turned and said to him, “Rabboni!” which is to say, “Teacher!”
slang_text:
They go, "Ma'am, what's got you so bummed out?"
She goes, "'Cause they took my Lord, and I got no clue where they stashed him." Soon as she says that, she spins around and spots Jesus just standing there, but she hasn't got a clue it's him, for real. Jesus goes,
"Ma'am, why the waterworks? Who you looking for?"
She figures he's gotta be the gardener, so she goes, "Sir, if you're the one who hauled him off, just clue me in where you put him and I'll go grab him." Jesus goes,
"Mary."
She spins back around and goes, "Rabboni!" -- which is basically like saying "Teacher!"

web_text:
Jesus said to her,
“Don’t hold me, for I haven’t yet ascended to my Father; but go to my brothers, and tell them, ‘I am ascending to my Father and your Father, to my God and your God.’” Mary Magdalene came and told the disciples that she had seen the Lord, and that he had said these things to her. When therefore it was evening, on that day, the first day of the week, and when the doors were locked where the disciples were assembled, for fear of the Jews, Jesus came and stood in the middle, and said to them,
“Peace be to you.” When he had said this, he showed them his hands and his side. The disciples therefore were glad when they saw the Lord.
slang_text:
Jesus goes,
"Don't cling onto me like that, 'cause I haven't cruised on up to my Father yet. But bounce over to my brothers and tell 'em, 'I'm heading up to my Father and your Father, to my God and your God.'" So Mary Magdalene goes and tells the disciples she saw the Lord, no lie, and that he laid all that on her. So then that evening, same day, first day of the week, with the doors bolted tight 'cause everybody's spooked about the Jews, Jesus just materializes, plants himself right in the middle of 'em, and goes,
"Peace be with you." Soon as he says it, he shows 'em his hands and his side. And the disciples go totally stoked when they clock the Lord.

web_text:
Jesus therefore said to them again,
“Peace be to you. As the Father has sent me, even so I send you.” When he had said this, he breathed on them, and said to them,
“Receive the Holy Spirit! If you forgive anyone’s sins, they have been forgiven them. If you retain anyone’s sins, they have been retained.” But Thomas, one of the twelve, called Didymus, wasn’t with them when Jesus came.
slang_text:
So Jesus lays it on 'em again,
"Peace be with you. Just like the Father sent me, I'm sending you." Then he breathes on 'em and goes,
"Receive the Holy Spirit! Check it -- if you let anybody's sins slide, they're forgiven, fer sure. If you hang onto anybody's sins, they stay hung onto." But Thomas, one of the twelve, the dude they called Didymus, wasn't around when Jesus rolled up.

web_text:
The other disciples therefore said to him, “We have seen the Lord!”
But he said to them, “Unless I see in his hands the print of the nails, put my finger into the print of the nails, and put my hand into his side, I will not believe.” After eight days again his disciples were inside, and Thomas was with them. Jesus came, the doors being locked, and stood in the middle, and said,
“Peace be to you.” Then he said to Thomas,
“Reach here your finger, and see my hands. Reach here your hand, and put it into my side. Don’t be unbelieving, but believing.” Thomas answered him, “My Lord and my God!”
slang_text:
So the other disciples are hyping him up, "We saw the Lord, for real!"
But he goes, "Nah, unless I see the nail marks in his hands with my own two eyes, jam my finger right into those marks, and shove my hand into his side, I ain't buying it, no way." Eight days later, the disciples are chillin' inside again, and this time Thomas is with 'em. Doors locked up tight, and Jesus just materializes, plants himself in the middle, and goes,
"Peace be with you." Then he turns to Thomas and goes,
"Bring your finger over here, check out my hands. Bring your hand here, shove it into my side. Quit trippin' and start believing." Thomas goes, "My Lord and my God!"

web_text:
Jesus said to him,
“Because you have seen me,
you have believed. Blessed are those who have not seen, and have believed.” Therefore Jesus did many other signs in the presence of his disciples, which are not written in this book; but these are written, that you may believe that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God, and that believing you may have life in his name.
slang_text:
Jesus goes to him,
"'Cause you saw me,
you believed. But the ones who never saw and still believe -- those cats are the truly blessed ones, for real." So Jesus busted out a ton of other epic signs right in front of his disciples that didn't even make the cut for this book. But these ones got written down so you'd believe Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God, and so that believing, you'd score life in his name.

web_text:
After these things, Jesus revealed himself again to the disciples at the sea of Tiberias. He revealed himself this way. Simon Peter, Thomas called Didymus, Nathanael of Cana in Galilee, and the sons of Zebedee, and two others of his disciples were together. Simon Peter said to them, “I’m going fishing.”
They told him, “We are also coming with you.” They immediately went out, and entered into the boat. That night, they caught nothing. But when day had already come, Jesus stood on the beach, yet the disciples didn’t know that it was Jesus.
slang_text:
After all that, Jesus popped up again to the disciples, out by the Sea of Tiberias. Here's the scoop on how it went down. Simon Peter, Thomas (the dude they called "the Twin"), Nathanael from Cana over in Galilee, Zebedee's boys, and a couple other disciples were all posted up together. Simon Peter goes, "I'm outta here, gonna go fishing."

They're like, "Fer sure, we're rollin' with you." So they bounced, piled in the boat, and headed out. But that whole night? Caught zip, nada, totally grody luck. Then when the sun finally came up, Jesus was just standing there on the beach, but the disciples were trippin' -- didn't have a clue it was Him.

web_text:
Jesus therefore said to them,
“Children, have you anything to eat?”

They answered him, “No.” He said to them,
“Cast the net on the right side of the boat, and you will find some.”
They cast it therefore, and now they weren’t able to draw it in for the multitude of fish. That disciple therefore whom Jesus loved said to Peter, “It’s the Lord!”
So when Simon Peter heard that it was the Lord, he wrapped his coat around him (for he was naked), and threw himself into the sea. But the other disciples came in the little boat (for they were not far from the land, but about two hundred cubits away), dragging the net full of fish.
slang_text:
So Jesus hollers over to 'em,
"Yo, kiddos, you nab anything to eat?"

They shout back, "Nope, bogus, nothin'." So He goes,
"Chuck the net on the right side of the boat, and you'll hit the jackpot."
So they slung it out there, and get this -- so many fish they couldn't even haul the thing in, it was epic! The disciple Jesus was tight with spins around to Peter and goes, "Dude, it's the Lord!"
Soon as Simon Peter heard that, he yanked his coat on quick as a wink (guy was buck naked) and just cannonballed straight into the sea, no joke. The rest of the crew came paddling in with the little boat -- they weren't far from shore, like two hundred cubits out -- haulin' that net stuffed to the gills with fish.

web_text:
So when they got out on the land, they saw a fire of coals there, and fish laid on it, and bread. Jesus said to them,
“Bring some of the fish which you have just caught.” Simon Peter went up, and drew the net to land, full of great fish, one hundred fifty-three; and even though there were so many, the net wasn’t torn. Jesus said to them,
“Come and eat breakfast.”
None of the disciples dared inquire of him, “Who are you?” knowing that it was the Lord.
slang_text:
So they hit the shore, and check it -- there's a charcoal fire already crackling, with fish sizzlin' on it, plus bread. Jesus goes,
"Bring some of that fish you dudes just reeled in." Simon Peter booked it over and dragged the net to shore, packed with monster fish, a hundred fifty-three of 'em -- and even with that many, the net held together like a boss, not one rip. Jesus goes,
"C'mon and eat some breakfast."
Not one disciple had the nerve to ask Him, "So, who are you, anyway?" 'Cause it was no duh -- they knew fer sure it was the Lord.

web_text:
Then Jesus came and took the bread, gave it to them, and the fish likewise. This is now the third time that Jesus was revealed to his disciples, after he had risen from the dead. So when they had eaten their breakfast, Jesus said to Simon Peter,
“Simon, son of Jonah, do you love me more than these?”
He said to him, “Yes, Lord; you know that I have affection for you.”
He said to him,
“Feed my lambs.” He said to him again a second time,
“Simon, son of Jonah, do you love me?”
He said to him, “Yes, Lord; you know that I have affection for you.”
He said to him,
“Tend my sheep.”
slang_text:
Then Jesus comes over, picks up the bread, passes it around to 'em, and does the same with the fish. This right here was the third time Jesus showed Himself to the disciples since He rose from the dead. So after they polished off breakfast, Jesus turns to Simon Peter and asks,
"Simon, son of Jonah, you dig me more than these other guys do?"
Peter's like, "Fer sure, Lord, you know I care about you."
Jesus goes,
"Feed my lambs." Then He comes at him again, second time around,
"Simon, son of Jonah, you dig me?"
Peter goes, "Fer sure, Lord, you know I care about you."
Jesus goes,
"Look after my sheep."

web_text:
He said to him the third time,
“Simon, son of Jonah, do you have affection for me?”
Peter was grieved because he asked him the third time,
“Do you have affection for me?” He said to him, “Lord, you know everything. You know that I have affection for you.”
Jesus said to him,
“Feed my sheep. Most certainly I tell you, when you were young, you dressed yourself, and walked where you wanted to. But when you are old, you will stretch out your hands, and another will dress you, and carry you where you don’t want to go.” Now he said this, signifying by what kind of death he would glorify God. When he had said this, he said to him,
“Follow me.” Then Peter, turning around, saw a disciple following. This was the disciple whom Jesus loved, the one who had also leaned on Jesus’ breast at the supper and asked, “Lord, who is going to betray You?”
slang_text:
Third time around He asks him,
"Simon, son of Jonah, you care about me?"
Peter's heart just sinks, way harsh, 'cause Jesus asked him a third time,
"You care about me?" So he goes, "Lord, you know everything, no lie. You know I care about you."
Jesus goes,
"Feed my sheep. For real, I'm telling you this straight up -- back when you were young, you dressed yourself and went wherever you pleased, easy breezy. But when you're old, you're gonna stretch your hands out, and somebody else is gonna dress you and drag you off somewhere you don't wanna go." He said this to clue Peter in on what kind of death would bring glory to God. After droppin' that bomb, He goes,
"Follow me." Then Peter spins around and spots another disciple trailin' behind -- the one Jesus was tight with, the same dude who leaned back on Jesus at the supper and asked, "Lord, who's gonna sell you out?"

web_text:
Peter seeing him, said to Jesus, “Lord, what about this man?” Jesus said to him,
“If I desire that he stay until I come, what is that to you? You follow me.” This saying therefore went out among the brothers, that this disciple wouldn’t die. Yet Jesus didn’t say to him that he wouldn’t die, but,
“If I desire that he stay until I come, what is that to you?” This is the disciple who testifies about these things, and wrote these things. We know that his witness is true.
slang_text:
Peter spots him and goes, "Lord, what's the deal with this guy, though?" Jesus goes,
"If I want him hangin' around till I come back, what's it to you? Just you, follow me." So word spread among the brothers that this disciple wasn't gonna kick it. But hold up -- Jesus never actually said he wouldn't die, He just said,
"If I want him hangin' around till I come back, what's it to you?" This is the same disciple who's layin' it all out about this stuff and wrote it down himself. And we know it's fer sure -- his word is legit.

web_text: There are also many other things which Jesus did, which if they would all be written, I suppose that even the world itself wouldn’t have room for the books that would be written.
slang_text: There's a bodacious ton of other stuff Jesus did too, and honestly, if somebody tried to write it all down, I bet the whole world wouldn't have enough room to stash all them books, no lie.
